title: Readiness, liveness, and resource requests/limits
summary: Concrete YAML patterns for probes and resources that satisfy baseline Kubernetes policy.
body:
Health probes let the kubelet attach routing and restart policy to application readiness. Resource requests enable scheduling; limits cap noisy-neighbor risk. Together they are required for predictable operations.

Define probes with timeouts and thresholds appropriate to your SLOs. Avoid copying generic 1s/1s values from tutorials without validating against startup time.

steps: Under spec.template.spec.containers[].readinessProbe, set httpGet or exec with initialDelaySeconds tuned to cold-start.
Add livenessProbe with stricter failure thresholds than readiness where appropriate.
Set resources.requests and resources.limits for cpu and memory on every container.
Re-run kubectl apply and confirm Ready replicas match desired.
remediation.example_manifest_snippet:
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "100m"
    memory: "128Mi"
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
readinessProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /readyz
    port: 8080
  initialDelaySeconds: 5
livenessProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /healthz
    port: 8080
  initialDelaySeconds: 15

nist.name: Contingency Plan
nist.discussion: Probes support controlled failover and recovery behaviors.

fedramp.control: SI-4 (System Monitoring) — workload signals for availability
fedramp.notes: Map probe failures to monitoring/alerting in the SSP.
